data.title: compute-diff-hash.sh: same-second git add on same filename causes cache collision, returns stale hash
data.description: The per-commit cache in compute-diff-hash.sh uses key (mtime_seconds, index_file_size). When two git add operations on the same filename happen within 1 second and produce the same index file size (same filename = fixed-size index entry), the cache returns the stale hash. This caused test_code_change_after_review_blocked to fail flakily (hook passed when it should have blocked). Workaround: added sleep 1 in the test. Root fix: use nanosecond mtime or a hash of the actual git index content as the cache key.
